reason: Codex runtime returned a usage-limit error (rate limited until 2026-06-20) when invoked this run. Fell back to a careful manual re-verification instead. The three deliverables target the UNSUPERVISED ANOMALY DETECTION (reconstruction) baseline. Verified every equation and the final code against retrieved canonical sources: thuml Time-Series-Library models/TimesNet.py Model.anomaly_detection (instance norm with torch.var unbiased=False +1e-5, both stats detached; enc_embedding(x_enc, None); residual layer_norm(TimesBlock) loop; projection Linear(d_model->c_out); de-normalize over seq_len; no predict_linear, pred_len=0, returns the reconstruction at input length), FFT_for_Period (rfft dim=1, amplitude mean over batch and channels, drop DC index 0, topk, period=T//f), TimesBlock forward (pad to multiple of period, reshape [B, length//period, period, N], permute 0,3,1,2, shared conv, permute back 0,2,3,1, softmax(amplitude) aggregation, residual), and Inception_Block_V1 (kernel_size=2i+1, padding=i, stack+mean over kernels). Axis labels follow the paper (columns=intraperiod within a period, rows=interperiod same phase across periods), correcting the row/col swap in one third-party explainer.
